title: Equivariant Networks, Live
tagline: Rotate the input — the right architecture rotates the output too. Vanilla CNNs don't.
summary: Rotate a synthetic input image; compare the responses of a vanilla CNN, an augmented CNN, and a rotation-equivariant network. Watch the rotation-equivariant net produce a clean orbit while the vanilla CNN's response thrashes. Cross-link to satellite + molecular DL applications.
highlights: Slide the input rotation θ; watch the three architectures' responses to a rotated airplane silhouette.
Vanilla CNN: response jumps. Augmented CNN: smooths but still wobbles. C4 group conv: clean orbit.
Equivariance vs invariance: when you need each.
Beyond rotations: scale, gauge, SE(3), spherical, graph automorphisms.
